title: Power Up for National Battery Day!
story: Every February 18, National Battery Day celebrates the tiny powerhouses that keep our world running! People share photos of their favorite battery-powered gadgets, from remote controls to smartphones. Some families even do fun experiments, like making batteries from lemons or potatoes. It's the perfect day to appreciate how these small energy sources power everything from toys to flashlights, making our daily lives so much easier.
activity_question: What do some families do on National Battery Day to learn about batteries?
activity_choices: Make batteries from lemons or potatoes
activity_answer: Make batteries from lemons or potatoes
follow_up_question: What would happen if all the batteries in your house stopped working for a day?

title: Fishy Business Gets Crabby
story: Chefs across America get creative on February 18 with National Crab Stuffed Flounder Day! This tasty holiday celebrates a fancy dish where sweet crab meat gets stuffed inside tender flounder fish. Restaurants offer special menus featuring this seafood combo, while home cooks try their hand at making it from scratch. Food bloggers share photos of their creations online, and cooking shows often feature step-by-step recipes on this day.
activity_question: What gets stuffed inside the flounder on this holiday?
bonus_fact: Flounder are flat fish that swim sideways with both eyes on one side!

title: Pluto Gets Its Day in Space
story: Every February 18, space fans celebrate Pluto Discovery Day to remember when this tiny world was first spotted through a telescope! People mark this special day by sharing cool Pluto facts online, visiting planetariums, and learning about our solar system's most famous dwarf planet. Kids love posting pictures of Pluto with its heart-shaped marking, while astronomy clubs host stargazing parties. Some families even eat "planet cookies" shaped like spheres to honor all nine objects that orbit our sun. It's the perfect day to appreciate how much we've learned about space exploration and the amazing worlds beyond Earth.
activity_question: What do people often share online on Pluto Discovery Day?
bonus_fact: Pluto is so far away that sunlight takes over 5 hours to reach it!

title: Sky-High Moo: Flying Cow Milking Day
story: Every February 18, people celebrate one of the weirdest aviation records ever! This silly holiday remembers when someone actually milked a cow while flying in an airplane high above the clouds. Today, dairy farmers and aviation fans share funny memes and jokes about flying cows online. Some people visit air shows or dairy farms to celebrate both flying and farming. Kids love drawing pictures of cows with wings or airplanes carrying milk cartons. It's become a day to appreciate how creative and determined people can be when they want to set unusual records. Who knew cows and airplanes could make such a memorable combination?
activity_question: What do people share online to celebrate this unusual holiday?
activity_choices: Funny memes and jokes about flying cows
activity_answer: Funny memes and jokes about flying cows
bonus_fact: The cow was reportedly very calm during the entire flight!

title: Going Nuts for National Almond Day!
story: Crunch time arrives every February 18 when almond lovers everywhere celebrate National Almond Day! People munch on raw almonds, bake almond cookies, and sip almond milk to honor these tasty tree nuts. Restaurants add almond-crusted chicken to their menus, while bakeries showcase almond croissants and marzipan treats. Social media fills with photos of almond butter toast and homemade almond granola as families discover new nutty recipes together.
activity_question: What are some ways people celebrate National Almond Day?
activity_choices: Eating almond treats and trying new almond recipes
Planting almond trees in their backyards
Wearing brown clothes to match almond shells
activity_answer: Eating almond treats and trying new almond recipes
bonus_fact: California grows about 80% of the world's almonds!
